title: gynochthodes citrina
intro: Gynochthodes citrina, also known as the yellow shrub or yellow mangosteen, is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Gynochthodes citrina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its medicinal uses, but proper identification and preparation are crucial to ensure its safety and efficacy.
